title: Mellow Smoky Vietnamese Beef Pho
description: A smoky and juicy Vietnamese dish featuring beef stock concentrate and ferment-jang, this pho is a jain-friendly take on the classic recipe. It combines the deep flavors of a slow-cooked broth with tender beef and fresh herbs.
instructions: In a large pot, combine beef stock concentrate, ferment-jang, sliced onion, ginger, star anise, and cinnamon stick.
Pour in 4 quarts of water and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1 hour.
Add the sliced beef brisket to the pot and cook for an additional 30 minutes.
Cook the rice noodles according to the package instructions.
Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the solids.
Add the cooked noodles to the pot and cook for 2 minutes.
Serve the pho hot, with lime wedges on the side.
